# Crush — Full Documentation (v0.8.1)

> Crush is a from-scratch container runtime written in Rust. It runs your project natively on Windows (no WSL2, no Docker daemon, no VMs), auto-detects your stack, starts native dependencies, and cleans up via Windows Job Objects. For production, `crush eject` generates a real Dockerfile + docker-compose.yml.

---

## What Crush actually does

You `cd` into a project. You type `crush`. Crush:

1. Detects the stack — language, framework, port, monorepo structure. Precedence: Crushfile > compose > Dockerfile > heuristics. Files annotated `# crush:eject` are skipped.
2. Auto-starts the deps — native PostgreSQL, Garnet (Redis-compat), MySQL by parsing docker-compose.yml, application.yml, or .env. Per-service env merge for monorepos.
3. Picks the right entry command — `mvn spring-boot:run` if no Dockerfile, `pnpm dev` for standalone frontends, `pnpm start` for monorepos with a compose file.
4. Skips work already done — `pnpm install` is skipped when node_modules is newer than lockfile; image repack is skipped when source fingerprints match.
5. Scans for secrets before building — AWS, GCP, GitHub PAT, OpenAI, GitLab, npm, Vercel, Cloudflare tokens; DB connection strings; high-entropy strings. Lines annotated `# crush:ignore-secret` are skipped.
6. Surfaces URLs — probes /swagger-ui/index.html, /v3/api-docs, /actuator/health and prints them.
7. Kills the full process tree on Ctrl+C via Windows Job Object — no orphan processes holding ports.

Crush is NOT a Docker Desktop replacement. It does not run Linux containers on Windows. Your app runs as a native process. Use `crush eject` to get a real Dockerfile for production deployment.

---

## Installation

### Linux / macOS (curl)
```
curl -fsSL https://crush-web-six.vercel.app/install.sh | sh
```

### Windows (PowerShell)
```
irm https://crush-web-six.vercel.app/install.ps1 | iex
```

### Direct download
Download from https://github.com/Chidi09/crush/releases/latest
- Linux: crush-v0.8.1-linux-x86_64 (binary), Crush_0.8.1_amd64.deb, Crush_0.8.1_amd64.AppImage
- Windows: crush-windows-x86_64.exe (rename to crush.exe, place on PATH)

### From source
```
git clone https://github.com/Chidi09/crush.git
cd crush
cargo build --release -p crush-cli
```
Note: do NOT use --all-features (the ebpf feature requires nightly + bpf-linker).

### Package managers
APT, DNF, AUR, Winget, and Scoop packages are planned but not yet published.

---

## CLI Reference

### crush (default)
Detect stack, start deps, build, run — all in one command.
```
crush [--rebuild] [--repack] [--watch] [--memory 1G] [--cpus 0.5] [--platform linux/amd64]
```

### crush detect
Print what Crush detects about the current project without building.
```
crush detect
crush detect --json
```

### crush build
Build a Crush image.
```
crush build [--tag myapp:latest] [--platform linux/amd64] [--rebuild] [--repack]
```

### crush run
Run a previously-built image.
```
crush run <image> [--port 3000] [-d] [--restart always] [--memory 512M]
```

### crush eject
Write a real Dockerfile + docker-compose.yml. Generated files are marked `# crush:eject` so they don't loop back into detection.
```
crush eject [--out ./deploy]
```

### crush ps
List running containers.
```
crush ps [-a]
crush ps --format json
```

### crush logs
Stream or tail container logs.
```
crush logs <container-id> [-f] [--tail 100]
```

### crush stop
Stop a container and its process tree.
```
crush stop <container-id>
```

### crush inspect
Show detailed metadata for a container or image.
```
crush inspect <container-id|image-ref>
```

### crush stats
Live TUI dashboard (CPU + memory sparklines).
```
crush stats
```

### crush debug
AI crash diagnosis via Claude. Requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY.
```
crush debug <container-id>
```

### crush watch
Hot-restart on source change. On Windows use the --watch flag; on Linux you can use this subcommand.
```
crush --watch          # Windows
crush watch            # Linux
```

### crush images
List locally-stored images.
```
crush images
crush images --format json
```

### crush pull / crush push
Pull or push OCI images.
```
crush pull <registry>/<repo>:<tag>
crush push <registry>/<repo>:<tag>
```

### crush compose
Multi-service applications from docker-compose.yml.
```
crush compose up [-d]
crush compose down
crush compose ps
```

### crush services
Inspect and control native dependency processes.
```
crush services ps
crush services ps --format json
crush services ps --all-projects
crush services stop <name>
```

### crush history
Recent build outcomes.
```
crush history
crush history --format json
```

### crush migrate
Convert an existing Dockerfile into a Crushfile.
```
crush migrate [--dockerfile ./Dockerfile]
```

### crush secrets
Manage AES-256-GCM encrypted secrets.
```
crush secrets set <KEY> <value>
crush secrets list
crush secrets remove <KEY>
crush secrets export --to vault
```

### crush scan / crush sbom
Vulnerability scan and SBOM generation.
```
crush scan <image>
crush sbom <image> [--format spdx-json]
```

### crush network / crush volume
Container networks and persistent volumes.
```
crush network create <name>
crush network ls
crush volume create <name>
crush volume ls
```

### crush deploy / crush rollback
Deploy to a remote host and roll back.
```
crush deploy [--host user@server] [--image myapp:latest]
crush rollback [--host user@server]
```

### crush update
Self-update from GitHub releases.
```
crush update [--version v0.8.1]
```

### crush system
System info and maintenance.
```
crush system info
crush system prune
```

### crush completions
Shell completions.
```
crush completions bash
crush completions zsh
crush completions fish
crush completions powershell
```

### Global flags
- `--rebuild` — bust warm-run cache; re-run install + build steps
- `--repack` — force image re-pack even if source fingerprints match
- `--watch` — hot-restart on source file changes
- `--memory <limit>` — memory cap e.g. 512M or 2G
- `--cpus <n>` — CPU limit e.g. 0.5 for half a core
- `--priority <level>` — low | normal | high (Windows scheduling priority)
- `--platform <target>` — e.g. linux/amd64 or linux/arm64
- `--no-proxy` — skip the reverse proxy for monorepos

---

## Crushfile Schema (TOML)

The Crushfile is optional — Crush auto-detects everything. Use it to override or pin settings.

```toml
[project]
name = "my-api"
version = "0.1.0"

[build]
platform = "linux/amd64"          # target platform
base_image = "node:20-alpine"     # override auto-detected base
cmd = ["node", "dist/index.js"]   # override default entry command
expose = [3000]                   # ports to expose
env = { NODE_ENV = "production" } # build-time env vars

[runtime]
restart = "no"        # no | always | on-failure
memory = "512MB"
cpu = 0.5

[secrets]
# Set with: crush secrets set DB_PASSWORD <value>
# DO NOT put plaintext values here — use crush secrets set
```

Crushfile takes precedence over docker-compose.yml, which takes precedence over a Dockerfile, which takes precedence over heuristic detection. Files with `# crush:eject` in the first line are excluded from detection (used by `crush eject` to mark generated files).

---

## Stack Detection

Supported stacks (zero-config):

**Node.js / TypeScript**: Next.js, Nuxt, Vite, SvelteKit, AnalogJS, Angular, Astro, Express, Fastify, NestJS, Hono, Qwik
**Java**: Spring Boot, Quarkus, Micronaut (Maven and Gradle)
**Python**: FastAPI, Django, Flask, Litestar, Starlette
**Other**: Go, Rust, Ruby on Rails, PHP (Laravel), Elixir (Phoenix), .NET
**Monorepos**: Turborepo, Nx, pnpm-workspace, Lerna

Dockerfile detection searches: root, infra/, docker/, .docker/, deploy/, ops/
Dockerfile names matched: Dockerfile, dockerfile, Containerfile

---

## Secret Scanning

Runs automatically at build time before any code executes.

Detected patterns:
- AWS: access key IDs, secret keys
- GCP: service account keys
- GitHub: personal access tokens (ghp_, gho_, ghs_, ghr_ prefixes)
- OpenAI project keys
- GitLab personal access tokens (glpat- prefix)
- npm auth tokens
- Vercel tokens
- Cloudflare API tokens
- Database connection strings: postgresql://, mysql://, mongodb://, redis://, amqps:// with embedded credentials
- High-entropy strings: threshold 3.3 for hex, 4.5 for other charsets
- Hex keys with 64-char boundary guards

Escape hatch: annotate any line with `# crush:ignore-secret` to skip it.

Env var scanning: reads .env.example, .env.sample, .env.template, .env.dist for required vs optional classification. Code references scanned: process.env (JS), os.environ (Python), std::env::var (Rust), System.getenv (Java), ENV[] (Ruby), Deno.env.get (Deno).

---

## Security

Secrets are stored with AES-256-GCM encryption and decrypted only in memory at container runtime. They are injected as environment variables and never written to disk in plaintext.

Release binaries are distributed with SHA256SUMS for verification.

---

## Windows Job Objects

Crush uses the Win32 CreateJobObjectW / AssignProcessToJobObject APIs to group all spawned processes into a single job. When `crush` exits (including on Ctrl+C), the entire job is terminated — no orphan node, java, or postgres processes left holding ports.

Resource limits (--memory, --cpus) are enforced via SetInformationJobObject with JOB_OBJECT_LIMIT_WORKING_SET_SIZE and JobObjectCpuRateControlInformation.

This is the isolation mechanism. Crush does NOT use Linux namespaces, cgroups, or any hypervisor on Windows.

---

## Architecture

Crush is a Cargo workspace. Active crates (daily-driven):
- crush-cli: CLI entry, all subcommands
- crush-build: stack detection, Dockerfile/compose parsing, secrets & env scanning, build engine, RunEvent types
- crush-types: shared structs, dirs_or_default(), Container, Image
- crush-services: native postgres/redis/mysql, pgvector extension
- crush-image: OCI image store + sled-backed metadata
- crush-ai: Anthropic-backed crash diagnosis
- crush-tui: ratatui-based stats dashboard
- crush-runtime-windows: Job Objects, ConPTY
- crush-gui/src-tauri: Tauri 2 + Svelte desktop app

Scaffolded (not yet daily-driven):
- crush-runtime-linux, crush-runtime-macos, crush-runtime-wasm
- crush-network (eBPF feature gated behind ebpf feature flag, needs aya-ebpf port)
- crush-registry, crush-reliability, crush-volume, crush-deploy

---

## What's not implemented yet

- crush update SHA256 verification (self-update works but doesn't verify the downloaded binary)
- crush images "0 B" size display (images list but sizes show zero)
- Full crush compose lifecycle (starts deps, doesn't manage up/down/scale fully)
- crush watch subcommand on Windows (use --watch flag instead)
- Linux/macOS/WASM runtimes (crates exist, not battle-tested)
- APT/DNF/AUR/Winget/Scoop packages (planned, not published)

---

## Changelog highlights

### v0.8.1 (2026-06-12)
Version bump only — resolves tag collision with earlier v0.8.0 from a different branch.

### v0.8.0 (2026-06-12)
- Desktop GUI ships: Tauri 2 + Svelte. Real-time run events, working abort, batched log replay, dev mode toggle.
- Build engine: Crushfile > compose > Dockerfile > heuristics precedence. Dockerfile detection in nested dirs.
- Secrets scanner: multi-tier, charset-aware entropy, 10+ token pattern families.
- Env scanning: .env.example variants + code reference scanning.
- CLI: commands split into modules, StatelessEngine import fixed.
- Shared dirs_or_default() in crush-types.
- Local CI/release pipeline: scripts/ci-local.sh + release-local.sh, post-receive hook.

### v0.7.74 (2026-05-28)
Restored four output lines dropped by v0.7.73 refactor: dependencies cached, crushed to image, deps fresh, warm run icon.

---

https://github.com/Chidi09/crush
